name: Pamplona, Badostain, Sagaseta, Elkano Azpa, Labiano, Balsa Ezkoriz – Circular Route
name_original: Pamplona, Badostain, Sagaseta, Elkano Azpa, Labiano, Balsa Ezkoriz – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around Aranguren offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural landscapes. The region features a mix of paved roads connecting small towns and villages, alongside paths that traverse more open countryside. Elevations vary, providing both gentle stretches and more challenging climbs for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Aranguren The most popular touring cycling route is Pamplona, Badostain, Sagaseta, Elkano Azpa, Labiano, Balsa Ezkoriz – Circular Route, a 28.9 miles (46.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route connects several villages and features significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lamiturri Spring loop from Burlada / Burlata, a difficult 20.4 miles (32.8 km) path. This route explores natural spring areas and starts from the town of Burlada. Local touring cyclists also love the Artica Viewpoint loop from Burlada / Burlata, an 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail leading through elevated areas with scenic views, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Touring cycling around Aranguren is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Aranguren are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 6400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aranguren's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Aranguren?
A: There are over 290 touring cycling routes around Aranguren, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 41 easy routes, 98 moderate routes, and 154 difficult routes for more experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Aranguren?
A: Yes, Aranguren offers 41 easy touring cycling routes that are generally suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler terrain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the scenic landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Aranguren?
A: Touring cycling around Aranguren features varied terrain, characterized by rolling hills, picturesque river valleys, and expansive agricultural landscapes. You'll find a mix of paved roads connecting charming small towns and villages, as well as paths that traverse more open countryside. Elevations vary, providing both gentle stretches and more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Aranguren?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Aranguren are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968495549/pamplona-badostain-sagaseta-elkano-azpa-labiano-balsa-ezkoriz-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona, Badostain, Sagaseta, Elkano Azpa, Labiano, Balsa Ezkoriz – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that connects several villages.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can visit along the touring routes?
A: Aranguren's touring routes often pass by beautiful natural and historical sites. You can explore areas with natural springs like on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lamiturri Spring loop from Burlada / Burlata&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy elevated areas with panoramic views, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40339040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artica Viewpoint loop from Burlada / Burlata&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable natural attractions in the wider region include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zokorena Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Diablozulo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or places of interest can I explore while touring cycling?
A: The region around Aranguren is rich in history and culture. You can cycle past significant landmarks such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4817230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Cathedral and the Citadel&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/654847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Monument at Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer opportunities to experience the local heritage.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Aranguren is home to 154 difficult touring cycling routes, designed for experienced riders seeking a challenge. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968495549/pamplona-badostain-sagaseta-elkano-azpa-labiano-balsa-ezkoriz-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona, Badostain, Sagaseta, Elkano Azpa, Labiano, Balsa Ezkoriz – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbing.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Aranguren?
A: The touring cycling routes in Aranguren are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.3 out of 5 stars from over 260 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the well-maintained paths, and the scenic beauty of the region, making it a popular destination with over 6,400 visitors using komoot to explore.

Q: What is the typical distance and duration for touring cycling routes in Aranguren?
A: Route distances vary significantly, from shorter, easy rides to longer, more challenging tours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24667596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent from Tajonar to Noáin – El Oasis de Carmelo loop from El Sadar Stadium&lt;/a&gt; is about 18.6 miles (30 km) and takes around 2 hours 40 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20518692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguachiquita Bridge – Gravel Path loop from Burlada / Burlata&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 29.3 miles (47 km).

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Aranguren?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Aranguren is generally during the spring and autumn months. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, though it can get warmer. Winter cycling is possible but may require more preparation for cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

Q: Are there opportunities for refreshments or cafes along the touring routes?
A: Many touring routes in Aranguren pass through or near small towns and villages, offering opportunities to stop for refreshments. While specific cafes are not listed for every route, the presence of communities along the paths means you can often find local establishments to refuel and rest.
tips: Beautiful tree-lined avenue that leads to the Aranguren Valley and Badostain.
A place where you can refill your water bottle.
A beautiful cross. It's worth stopping and admiring, and maybe taking a photo or two.

There are less than 28 inhabitants in this town in the Egüés Valley but it is an old manor and there is a palace of the Viscount of Azpa and Marquis of Fontellas. There is also an archery range located in the middle of a beautiful landscape of wheat fields and cereal fields.
We will be able to observe cereal fields and scrubland, pines, oaks, beech trees and some lime trees, rowan trees, maples, boxwood, genista and juniper.
Trash bin, benches with tables, nice view of the mountains. Good place to rest a while on the way.
Beautiful stone fountain to fill your bottle.
It is also known as the Zolina Raft. It is a very quiet place where you can see a large number of birds that live near the pond. Enjoy the magnificent views!
